summary: neon-terminal ships a remote-shell backdoor on module import
details: The package advertises itself as a tiny ANSI color / TTY helper, but the main entrypoint (cjs/src/index.js and the ESM copy) runs top-level code that connects to a hardcoded remote endpoint at https://tracker.bvgroup.co/remote/<token> the moment the module is require()'d or import()'d. On load it POSTs a host-identification payload (HOSTNAME, process.platform, arch, pid) to that endpoint, then enters an infinite polling loop that fetches attacker-supplied tasks, executes each task's command via child_process.exec, and POSTs stdout/stderr back to the same endpoint. This is an unauthenticated command-and-control backdoor that gives the operator of tracker.bvgroup.co arbitrary shell execution on any machine that imports the package, with host identity exfiltrated on first contact. The 'ANSI color helpers' framing and the _tracker=hello query parameter are cover for the C2 channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
